w:r.w:t: Adı: CEN306 — Mobile Application Design and Development
Students or Team Members Who Prepared the Project
(This section will be filled out by the Instructor.)
Flutter Mobile Application Project — Code Evaluation (70 Points)
Flutter Mobile Application Project — Project Report Evaluation (30 Points)
CEN306 - Mobile Application Design and Development
OMAR Chat — WhatsApp-Style Mobile Messaging Application
CEN306 - Mobile Application Design and Development
Academic Integrity Statement: I confirm that this report and project were prepared by me and that all external sources, libraries, code snippets, and datasets are properly acknowledged.

OMAR Chat is a Flutter messaging application inspired by WhatsApp, developed for the CEN306 course. It targets students and project teams who need direct and group chat, media sharing, status updates, and intelligent notification feedback (sound + text-to-speech). The app demonstrates layered architecture with Presentation, Domain, Data, and Services layers, SQLite schema with DAO/Repository pattern, and Cubit state management.

On the technical side, the project uses flutter_bloc (Cubit), sqflite-compatible schema with an in-memory store for web demo, local notifications, sound alerts, TTS announcements, group @mention auto-replies, and simulated voice/video calls with call history.
Need for a structured, feature-rich chat app demonstrating mobile architecture patterns
University students, project teams (demo user: OMAR)
Flutter/Dart, SQLite/sqflite, flutter_bloc, flutter_tts, just_audio, flutter_local_notifications
Full WhatsApp-style UX with sound/TTS alerts, @mention replies, 11 screens, and clean layered architecture

OMAR Chat was developed to demonstrate CEN306 learning outcomes: layered design, persistent data storage, state management, and polished UI comparable to modern messaging apps. It simulates a multi-user environment where OMAR (user ID 1) interacts with seeded contacts through direct chats, groups, status, and calls.

Included: direct/group messaging, message edit/delete with time windows, @mentions, sound + TTS notifications, search, status (24h), block/mute, themes, simulated calls, call history, 11 screens. Out of scope: real backend server, WebRTC, push notification server, end-to-end encryption.

A student opens OMAR Chat, sees unread badges on the chat list, opens a group chat, types "@Ahmed can we meet tomorrow?" — Ahmed replies contextually; OMAR hears a notification sound and TTS says "Ahmed sent you a message".
Text, voice, files, images, drawings, emoji
Alert tone and spoken sender name on incoming messages
Mentioned group members reply based on message content
Find users and messages globally or in-chat
Send/receive messages in direct and group chats
Edit (10 min) and delete messages (for all: 5 min)
Sound + TTS notification on incoming messages
SQLite schema with DAO and Repository
Search, block, mute, status, simulated calls
Green theme, chat list, bubbles, bottom input bar
Cubit + async DAO; in-memory store on web
Block checks, try/catch on services, empty input guards
UI / Domain / Data / Services — no DB calls from screens
Sound + TTS: "Ahmed sent you a message"

Material Design with WhatsApp-inspired palette: app bar #008069, accent #25D366, chat background #ECE5DD. Reusable widgets include ChatTile, MessageBubble, MentionText, ChatInputBar, EmojiPickerPanel, and UnreadBadge.
Splash → Chat List → Chat Detail / Search / Status / Profile / Settings / Create Group. Chat Detail → Call. User Detail → Message or Call. Named routes registered in app.dart.
Figure 1. Chat List — unread badges and navigation
Chat List is the main hub showing all direct chats and groups with unread counts, search, status, and menu access.
Figure 2. Direct Chat — messaging with Ahmed
Direct Chat supports text, attachments, reply, edit, delete, and triggers simulated auto-reply with sound/TTS alerts.
Group Chat allows @mentioning members; mentioned users reply contextually to the message content.
Figure 4. Settings — notifications, sound, and TTS toggles
Settings screen controls notifications, sound alerts, and text-to-speech announcements.
The project implements a strict four-layer architecture that separates concerns and ensures no screen calls the database directly:
Entities and use cases — business rules
The folder structure explicitly separates UI Layer, Business Layer, and Data Layer. No source code is included in this report — only structure and responsibilities.
Display data, capture user input, call use cases via Cubit
Enforce edit/delete windows, block/mute, notifications logic
Persist and retrieve data via DAO/Repository
6. Data Layer: SQLite, DAO, and Repository
Repositories aggregate DAOs and map models to domain entities. Screens and use cases call repositories only — never DAOs or SQL directly.
11 screens: Splash, Chat List, Chat Detail, Create Group, Search, Profile, Settings, User Detail, Status, Call, Call History. 18+ reusable widgets in lib/presentation/widgets/.
Named routes in app.dart with RouteSettings arguments. MultiBlocProvider at app root supplies Cubits to all screens.
flutter_bloc Cubits: ChatListCubit, ChatDetailCubit, SearchCubit, GroupCubit, ProfileCubit, SettingsCubit, CallHistoryCubit.
7.4 Validation, Error Handling, and User Feedback
7.5 Important Code Files (no source code in report)
Send with block check and unread update
Web demo persistence with seed data
All incoming alerts moved to ReceiveMessageUseCase only
CMake define for coroutine deprecation warning
Shared state across 11 screens; testable use cases
UI never touches SQL; easy storage backend swap
OMAR Chat meets CEN306 requirements with layered architecture, DAO/Repository, Cubit state management, 11 screens, sound/TTS notifications, and @mention auto-replies.
Firebase Auth or custom JWT API
